procedure_name: Special Neonatal Care Package: Babies that required admission to SNCU or NICU
clinical_indications: Babies admitted for short term care for conditions like:

clinical_thresholds.note: Silverman Anderson or Downes' Vidyasagar score for mild respiratory distress
American Academy of Pediatrics (AAP) nomogram for intensive phototherapy based on gestation, birth weight and postnatal age
Definition of hypoglycemia in all newborns

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings and planned line of management
Chest X-ray (Mandatory if Mild Respiratory Distress/tachypnea)
Hemoglobin (Mandatory if Mild Respiratory Distress/tachypnea)
Blood sugar (Mandatory if Mild Respiratory Distress/tachypnea)
Sepsis screen (Mandatory if Mild Respiratory Distress/tachypnea)
Blood culture (Mandatory if Mild Respiratory Distress/tachypnea)
2DECHO (Optional if Mild Respiratory Distress/tachypnea)
Arterial Blood Gas (ABG) analysis (Optional if Mild Respiratory Distress/tachypnea)
Arterial Blood Gas (ABG)/Cord blood analysis (Mandatory if Mild encephalopathy)
Blood sugar (Mandatory if Mild encephalopathy)
Serum Electrolytes (Mandatory if Mild encephalopathy)
Serum Creatinine (Mandatory if Mild encephalopathy)
Septic screen/CSF Analysis (Meningitis) (Optional if Mild encephalopathy)
Metabolic profile (for fatty acid defects) (Optional if Mild encephalopathy)
Liver function test (Mandatory if Severe jaundice requiring intensive phototherapy)
Coomb`s test (Direct) (Mandatory if Severe jaundice requiring intensive phototherapy)
Hemoglobin, reticulocyte count, peripheral smear for evidence of hemolysis (Mandatory if Severe jaundice requiring intensive phototherapy)
Blood grouping (mother and newborn) (Mandatory if Severe jaundice requiring intensive phototherapy)
Sepsis screen (Optional if Severe jaundice requiring intensive phototherapy)
G6PD enzyme activity (for boys) (Optional if Severe jaundice requiring intensive phototherapy)
Hearing assessment (BERA) (Optional if Severe jaundice requiring intensive phototherapy)
Complete blood count (Mandatory if Haemorrhagic disease of newborn)
Coagulation profile – Prothrombin Time, Activated Partial Thromboplastin Time (Mandatory if Haemorrhagic disease of newborn)
Sepsis screen (Mandatory if Haemorrhagic disease of newborn)
Liver Function Test (Optional if Haemorrhagic disease of newborn)
D-Dimer test (Optional if Haemorrhagic disease of newborn)
Ferritin (Optional if Haemorrhagic disease of newborn)
PIVKA (vitamin K deficiency) (Optional if Haemorrhagic disease of newborn)
Apt test (Optional if Haemorrhagic disease of newborn)
USG skull/abdomen (Optional if Haemorrhagic disease of newborn)
Blood sugar (Mandatory if Unwell baby requiring monitoring)
Serum Calcium (Mandatory if Unwell baby requiring monitoring)
Sepsis screen (Mandatory if Unwell baby requiring monitoring)
Serum Electrolytes (Mandatory if Unwell baby requiring monitoring)
Abdominal girth (Mandatory if Unwell baby requiring monitoring)
Neurosonogram (Optional if Unwell baby requiring monitoring)
Clinical evaluation (Mandatory if Some dehydration)
Serum electrolytes (Mandatory if Some dehydration)
Urine analysis (Mandatory if Some dehydration)
Blood sugar (Mandatory if Hypoglycaemia)
Sepsis screen (Optional if Hypoglycaemia)

claim.label: Detailed Procedure notes and indication (if any)

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, vitals, examination findings, planned line of treatment, and advice for admission consistent with clinical criteria?
Chest X-ray/sepsis screen indicative of mild respiratory distress diagnosis?
TSB levels plotted on nomogram and indicative of intensive phototherapy?
Complete blood count/septic screen/coagulation profile investigations done?
Monitoring charts (HR, RR, oxygenation, perfusion, temperature) documented?
Monitoring charts for any procedure done or on ventilatory support available?
Clinical evaluation/serum electrolyte suggestive of dehydration?
Blood sugar for at-risk neonates monitored and suggestive of hypoglycemia diagnosis?

cpd_claim.q: Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) with daily vitals and line of treatment available?
Detailed procedure notes with indication available (optional)?
Discharge summary with follow-up advice available?

common_queries: Clinical notes lacking evaluation findings, planned line of treatment, or admission advice.
Mandatory investigation (e.g., Chest X-ray, Sepsis screen, Blood sugar) missing or not indicative of the specific diagnosis for the admitted condition.
Respiratory severity score not documented for mild respiratory distress.
APGAR score, HIE score, or septic screen not documented for mild encephalopathy.
TSB levels not plotted on nomogram or not indicative of intensive phototherapy for severe jaundice.
Complete blood count, septic screen, or coagulation profile investigations not done for hemorrhagic disease of newborn.
Monitoring charts (HR, RR, oxygenation, perfusion, temperature) not documented for unwell baby.
Weight loss, decreased urine output, or increased urine specific gravity not documented for dehydration.
Blood sugar not documented below 45 mg/dl for hypoglycemia, or not documented at all.

additional_information.referral_criteria: If hypoglycemia persists despite above management, refer to a higher health facility for further evaluation and management.
